title: Recall Early Memories: - Use Triggers: Look at Photos or Listen to Music from
subtitle: Explore Childhood Amnesia
description:
To recall early memories:
- Use triggers: Look at photos or listen to music from your early years.
- Ask family: Talk to relatives about events you may have forgotten.
- Write it down: Jot down fragments of memories as they surface.

Example: A photo of a birthday party might help you recall more about the event.

howTo.title: Choose a sensory trigger
howTo.summary: Select a photo, song, or object from your early years to serve as a cue for memory recall.

howTo.title: Engage with the trigger
howTo.summary: Spend 20 minutes focusing on the chosen trigger. Note sensory details and emotions that arise during this time.

howTo.title: Write down your memories
howTo.summary: After engaging with the trigger, write a short paragraph capturing the details and feelings associated with the memory.

howTo.title: Ask family for input
howTo.summary: After your initial session, reach out to relatives for specific details that may enhance your memory recall.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/recall-early-childhood-memories-triggers/.
